name: SDLC Delivery Team
description: Seven pre-wired agents that cover software delivery end to end on one codebase: a solution architect scopes the work into a story-pointed plan, a dev clone implements that plan (writes the code + tests, runs them) and scans for backlog, a code reviewer guards quality, a security sentinel audits for vulnerabilities, a release manager versions and ships, a docs steward keeps the README and changelog in sync, and an artist generates on-brand UI/visual assets for the feature in parallel with the build. They hand work forward through events and share the same codebase and memory. (The artist needs an image-generation credential; without one it stays idle and the build cascade is unaffected.)

team.name: SDLC Delivery Team
team.description: Architect → implement → review → security → release → docs — end-to-end software lifecycle on one codebase.

group.name: Software Delivery
group.shared_instructions: Every member operates on the same registered codebase. Ground every assertion in codebase evidence — cite file:line rather than speculating. Hand work forward through events, not direct calls. Track decisions, findings, and version history in memory so the team's knowledge compounds across runs instead of resetting each time. Prefer empirical findings over assumptions; when a precondition (codebase access) is missing, say so explicitly rather than fabricating results.

connections.label: scoped, story-pointed task breakdown to implement (architecture.analysis.completed)
shipped + documented state for the next review cycle (docs.sync.completed)
scoped feature for UI/brand visual assets, in parallel with the build (architecture.analysis.completed)
